title: Ensure that the cluster enforces Pod Security Standard  Baseline profile or stricter for all namespaces.

input_row.title: Ensure that the cluster enforces Pod Security Standard  Baseline profile or stricter for all namespaces.
input_row.description: The Pod Security Standard Baseline profile defines a baseline for container security. You can enforce this by using the built-in Pod Security Admission controller.
input_row.rationale: Without an active mechanism to enforce the Pod Security Standard Baseline profile, it is not possible to limit the use of containers with access to underlying cluster nodes, via mechanisms like privileged containers, or the use of hostPath volume mounts.
input_row.audit: Run the following command to list the namespaces that don't have the the baseline policy enforced. diff \ <(kubectl get namespace -l pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=baseline -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\n"}') \ <(kubectl get namespace -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\n"}')
input_row.remediation: Ensure that Pod Security Admission is in place for every namespace which contains user workloads. Run the following command to enforce the Baseline profile in a namespace: kubectl label namespace pod- security.kubernetes.io/enforce=baseline
input_row.default_value: By default, Pod Security Admission is enabled but no policies are in place.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_cluster_pod_security_standard_baseline_enforcement

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program will perform the following checks using GCP API calls and Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) methods:

1. **GKE API Evaluation**: Use the GKE API to retrieve details about the Kubernetes cluster and check the configuration of the Pod Security Admission controller.
   
2. **GCP CLI Command**: Execute the command:
   ```
   kubectl get namespace -l pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=baseline -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{\"\\n\"}'
   ```
   This command will list all namespaces that have the Pod Security Standard Baseline profile enforced.

3. **List All Namespaces**: Run the command:
   ```
   kubectl get namespace -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{\"\\n\"}'
   ```
   This command lists all the namespaces in the cluster.

4. **Compare Output**: The program will compare the results from the two commands to identify any namespaces that do not have the Baseline profile enforced.

5. **Pass/Fail Criteria**:
   - **Pass**: All namespaces in the cluster have the label `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=baseline`.
   - **Fail**: One or more namespaces do not have the Baseline profile enforced.

6. **Expected Configuration Properties**:
   - The expected configuration is that every namespace containing user workloads must have the Pod Security Admission set to enforce the Baseline profile.

By ensuring that each namespace is compliant with the Pod Security Standard Baseline profile, the organization reduces the risk associated with the deployment of container workloads in the GKE environment.
